blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ adverse fetal exposures?
ทารกที่นอนอยู่ใน NICU ไม่ได้เริ่มต้นชีวิตที่ห้องคลอด — เขาเริ่มจาก สิ่งแวดล้อมของแม่ตั้งแต่ก่อนตั้งครรภ์ด้วยซ้ำ. ทุกสิ่งที่แม่สัมผัส — ควันบุหรี่, แอลกอฮอล์, สารกำจัดแมลง, BPA ในขวดพลาสติก, ตะกั่วในดิน, มลภาวะอากาศ — ล้วนสามารถผ่าน placenta มาถึงสมองที่กำลังพัฒนาได้. บทนี้สอน 5 สิ่ง: (1) DOHaD + epigenetics — กลไกที่ environment reprograms fetal genome; (2) pathways ที่ toxicants เข้าถึง fetus — concurrent/nonconcurrent, placenta-dependent/independent; (3) fetal pharmacokinetics — ทำไม fetus จึง vulnerable กว่า adult; (4) specific toxicants: smoke, ethanol/FASD, pesticides, BPA, phthalates, organic solvents; (5) prevention counseling ที่ใช้ได้จริงใน antenatal visit.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย DOHaD (Developmental Origins of Health and Disease) และ Barker hypothesis
อธิบาย epigenetic mechanisms: DNA methylation, histone modification, microRNA
ระบุ critical windows of vulnerability — periconceptional period คือ most vulnerable ที่สุด
อธิบาย epigenetic transgenerational inheritance — ตัวอย่าง DES, vinclozolin
แยกแยะ concurrent vs nonconcurrent exposures — PCBs, Lead mobilization during pregnancy
อธิบาย placenta-dependent pathways: passive diffusion, ion trapping, active transport (Lead via Ca channels)
อธิบาย placenta-independent pathways: ionizing radiation (lowest effect 1–9 rad), heat (≥2°C), noise (>65 dB <250 Hz)
อธิบาย fetal pharmacokinetics — dilutional hypoalbuminemia, fetal ion trapping, immature CYP450
ท่องจำ FAS diagnostic triad + FASD spectrum + no safe dose of alcohol at any trimester
จำแนก pesticide classes: organochlorine (GABA), organophosphate (AChE), pyrethroid, carbamate, neonicotinoid
อธิบาย BPA (estrogenic EDC) + phthalates (antiandrogenic EDC) — ผลต่อ fetal reproductive/brain development
ให้ prevention counseling เกี่ยวกับ plastic, tobacco, alcohol, pesticides, solvents ในฐานะ neonatologist
1. DOHaD และ Epigenetics — รากฐานของ Fetal Programming
1.1 Developmental Origins of Health and Disease (DOHaD)

DOHaD คือ framework ที่อธิบายว่า environmental stressors ในช่วง critical periods ของ fetal development สามารถ reprogram genetics และส่งผลต่อ health ตลอดชีวิต. เริ่มจาก Barker hypothesis (1990): British epidemiologist David Barker พบว่า intrauterine growth restriction → coronary artery disease ในผู้ใหญ่ ผ่าน fetal nutritional metabolic programming. ปัจจุบัน DOHaD ขยายครอบคลุม toxicant exposures.
Fetal genetic plasticity: intrauterine environment สามารถเปลี่ยน phenotypic expression โดยไม่แก้ DNA sequence — ผ่าน epigenetic mechanisms
Dutch Hunger Winter (1944–1945): offspring ที่แม่ขาดอาหาร → persistent ↓ methylation ของ IGF-2 loci → ↑ metabolic + cardiovascular disease ในผู้ใหญ่ (Stevenson Curr Opin Endo Metab Res 2020;13:1)
DOHaD ใน toxicant context: prenatal tobacco → ADHD/obesity; prenatal alcohol → intellectual delay; prenatal lead → cognitive delay at 24 months; prenatal OPs → autism spectrum disorder
1.2 Epigenetic Mechanisms — 3 ชนิดหลัก

rows: เพิ่ม methyl group ที่ cytosine (CpG sites) ใน promoter region → chromatin coils tightly → gene silencing; DNMT1 maintains; DNMT3a/b de novo methylation
Air pollution PM10 → changed methylation in cord blood WBCs (periconceptional period most sensitive); maternal smoking → 69 methylation sites in 36 genomic regions persist to adulthood
Acetylation/phosphorylation ที่ N-terminal tail ของ 8 nuclear proteins (nucleosome packs 146 bp DNA) → change nucleosome structure → activate or repress transcription
PAH (cigarette smoke) → PAH-DNA adducts → histone modification → cancer risk; fetus highly susceptible to PAH-DNA adduct formation
Small noncoding RNA → regulate post-transcriptional expression of mRNA → alter gene expression without changing DNA sequence
Pesticide/toxicant exposure → altered miRNA expression patterns → developmental abnormalities

blocks.text: Genomic Imprinting

Silencing ของ 1 parental allele → monoallelic expression; เกิดช่วง early development. Dysregulation → Angelman (maternal 15q), Prader-Willi (paternal 15q), Beckwith-Wiedemann — aberrant DNA methylation
Prader-Willi: seasonal incidence pattern → สัมพันธ์กับ seasonal occupational exposures (farming, landscaping, painting) → paternal hydrocarbon exposure + paternal 15q uniparental disomy

Critical Window: Periconceptional Period (Wks 1–4) — Most Vulnerable to Epigenetic Modification
หลัง fertilization: parental methylation erased; remethylation เกิดที่ ~1 week (implantation). ดังนั้น periconceptional exposure = vulnerable ที่สุด สำหรับ epigenetic modification. PM10 exposure ช่วงนี้ → greatest changes ใน telomere length + methylation in cord blood (Isaevska Environ Res 2022;209:112717). Clinical: counseling ต้องเริ่มก่อนตั้งครรภ์ — preconception visit เพราะ exposure ก่อนรู้ว่าท้องส่งผลต่อ epigenome แล้ว.
1.3 Epigenetic Transgenerational Inheritance
Definition: inheritance of epigenetic information (epimutations in germline) ข้ามรุ่น — generation ที่ไม่ได้สัมผัส toxicant โดยตรง ยัง express phenotypic abnormalities

DES (Diethylstilbestrol): estrogenic drug ให้ 5–10 ล้านผู้หญิงทั่วโลก 1940s–1970s. DES granddaughters (F3 generation): ↑ irregular menses, amenorrhea, preterm delivery แม้ไม่เคยสัมผัส DES เอง (Titus Reprod Toxicol 2019;84:32)
Vinclozolin (fungicide) + Methoxychlor (pesticide) rat model: impaired spermatogenesis + infertility ใน male ถึง 4 subsequent generations (Manikkam PLoS One 2013;8:e55387)
DEHP + DBP (phthalates) rat model: prenatal exposure → cryptorchidism, hypospadias, ↓ anogenital distance, ↓ sperm production ใน offspring
2. Timing of Exposure — Concurrent vs Nonconcurrent
2.1 Nonconcurrent Exposures — Maternal Body Burden

PCBs (Polychlorinated Biphenyls): lipophilic organochlorine EDCs; bioaccumulate ใน adipose tissue; POPs (persistent organic pollutants); banned แต่ยังพบใน breast milk + maternal blood. Prenatal → neurodevelopmental impairment, autism (Lyall Environ Health Perspect 2017;125:474). Yu-cheng disease Taiwan 1979: PCB rice oil poisoning → children born ≤6 years after epidemic มี ectodermal defects + developmental delay เท่ากับ acute exposure (Chen JAMA 1992;268:3213)

Lead: major repository = bone; calcium turnover during pregnancy → mobilizes lead → crosses placenta via calcium-specific channels. Maternal tibial + patellar bone lead → ↓ birth weight + ↓ head circumference. Trabecular bone lead = independent risk factor for cognitive delay at 24 months (Gomaa Pediatrics 2002;110:110). Calcium supplementation → ↓ lead mobilization → ↓ placental transfer
2.2 Concurrent Exposures
Environmental Working Group (2007): cord blood of 10 US newborns → 287 toxicants; average 200 chemicals/infant (pesticides, PCBs, mercury)
Meconium analysis Philippines (n=426): lead 26.5%, mercury 83.9%, OP malathion 53% (Ostrea Neurotoxicology 2002;23:329)

Occupation + paraoccupation: cleft lip/palate → hairdressers, leather workers; limb anomalies → agricultural workers; NTDs → cleaners + health workers (anesthesia/radiation). Paraoccupational OP exposure in farm worker families → ↑ neurocognitive impairment in children (Muñoz-Quezada Neurotoxicology 2013;39:158)

Climate change: heat waves, wildfires, flooding → ↑ preterm birth, ↓ birth weight, spontaneous abortion, neonatal death. Mechanisms: oxidative stress, inflammatory responses, endocrine disruption, malnutrition, psychological stress (Ha Curr Environ Health Rep 2022;9:263)
Air pollution (PM2.5, PM10, O₃, SO₂, NO₂): ↑ prematurity, ↓ birth weight, ↑ ASD, ↑ neuromotor impairment, impaired lung + immune function (Inoue Curr Environ Health Rep 2020;7:231)

Arsenic (drinking water): naturally occurring; crosses placenta; strongest association: ↑ spontaneous abortion; also ↑ stillbirth, ↓ birth weight, ↑ neonatal mortality (Quansah Environ Health Perspect 2015;123:412). High in Bangladesh, Taiwan, Chile

Methylmercury (fish/seafood): lipid-soluble neurotoxin; EPA oral reference dose 0.1 μg/kg/day; ~8% women exceed this. Prenatal → microcephaly, seizures, cognitive + sensory impairment (Karagas Environ Health Perspect 2012;120:799)

Diet + epigenome: Dutch famine → ↓ IGF-2 methylation; maternal obesity + GDM → altered placenta + cord blood methylation → metabolic disease in offspring; folate + B12 deficiency → ↑ homocysteine → CVD risk
3. Pathways of Fetal Exposure
3.1 Placenta-Dependent Pathways

rows: Concentration gradient; properties: ↓MW, lipophilicity, ↓protein binding, nonionized
PCBs, ethanol, PAHs, CO, organic solvents
Mimics nutrient transport via specific channels; against concentration gradient
Lead → via Ca-specific channels; AA analogs

blocks.text:
Ion Trapping ใน Fetal Compartment — ทำไม Fetus จึงสะสม Weak Bases มากกว่า Mother
Fetal pH (~7.35) < Maternal pH (~7.40). Weak base nonionized ในแม่ → passive diffusion ผ่าน placenta → ionized ใน fetal compartment → ไม่สามารถ diffuse back → trapped ใน fetus. ประกอบกับ fetal albumin เพิ่มขึ้น progressive ตาม GA → protein binding ใน fetal compartment → further accumulation. ก่อน 29 wks: minimal fetal adipose → lipid-soluble toxicants (DDT, PCBs) accumulate ใน brain แทน. DDT brain retention highest at neonatal equivalent of brain growth spurt → behavioral abnormalities (Eriksson Toxicol Lett 1984;22:323).
3.2 Placenta-Independent Pathways
Ionizing Radiation

Well-characterized teratogen; most harmful in 1st trimester. Hiroshima/Nagasaki survivors in utero <18 wks: microcephaly dose-dependent; lowest observable effect at 1–9 rad (Blot J Radiat Res 1975;16:82)
↑ Cancer incidence at ≥0.10 Gy in utero (Yoshimoto Lancet 1988;332:665). Cranial CT neonatal: ~10.5 rad → use sparingly; prefer US/MRI. UV light does NOT penetrate fetus
Heat
Maternal core temperature ≥2°C above baseline + duration = key risk factors → cardiac defects, ↑ miscarriage
Pregnancy-induced temperature regulation → protective; risk mainly from fever/heat exhaustion, not moderate exercise (Ravanelli Br J Sports Med 2019;53:799)
Noise
23–25 wks: physiologic changes in fetal state; 25–29 wks: auditory system functional. 27 wks: hears <500 Hz; >500 Hz not until ~29 wks
Sound Study Group: avoid <250 Hz >65 dB extended exposure; avoid headphones on maternal abdomen (no benefit + cochlear hair cell risk) (Krueger J Obstet Gynecol Neonatal Nurs 2012;41:166)
4. Fetal Pharmacokinetics — ทำไม Fetus จึง Vulnerable กว่า Adult
4.1 Absorption and Distribution
Maternal plasma volume ↑ 50% during pregnancy: dilutional hypoalbuminemia → ↑ unbound fraction of xenobiotics → ↑ placental transfer
↑ Progesterone → ↑ gastric emptying time + ↓ intestinal motility: ↓ peak xenobiotic levels BUT ↑ overall exposure duration
Amniotic fluid = 3rd compartment: ↓ protein + delayed elimination → prolonged exposure to unbound water-soluble chemicals; increases as fetal kidney matures
Fetal distribution: before 29 wks, minimal adipose → lipid-soluble toxicants concentrate in brain
4.2 Metabolism

Phase I (CYP450: oxidation, reduction, hydrolysis) + Phase II (glucuronidation, sulfation): 75% chemicals toxic in original form; 25% metabolized to toxic intermediates. Fetal enzymes mature with GA → poorly expressed early → fetus may be PROTECTED from prodrug-type toxicants. Example: acetaminophen → hepatotoxic metabolite via phase I; ↓ fetal CYP450 → relatively protected

Genetic polymorphisms: (1) ADH high-activity variant → faster ethanol elimination → ↓ FASD risk. (2) PON1 variants → variable OP detoxification; certain allele → ↓ Bayley MDI at 24 months (Engel Environ Health Perspect 2016;124:822)

NICU Excipient Exposure: Ethanol ใน Hand Sanitizer + IV Medications
Hsieh et al. Arch Dis Child Fetal Neonatal Ed 2017: measurable ethanol in NICU incubator air from hand sanitizers. Akinmboni et al. J Perinatol 2017: VLBW n=106: 98% exposed to excipients; 5–9% received doses >acceptable adult thresholds (benzyl alcohol, propylene glycol, ethanol). Action: review excipient content ของ IV medications ทุกชนิดก่อนให้ใน VLBW; allow hand sanitizer to evaporate before contact with infant.
5. Cigarette Smoke และ Environmental Tobacco Smoke (ETS)
5.1 Scope + Components
>2.3 million women worldwide/year smoke during pregnancy. ETS >250 chemicals; ≥69 carcinogens: CO, cyanide, benzene, arsenic, PAHs (benzo[a]pyrene). No safe level of ETS exposure
Cotinine (nicotine metabolite, t½ ~20 h) = biomarker for ETS

Vaping: nicotine + propylene glycol + flavorings aerosolized. Recent studies: no clear ↑ LBW from vaping vs smoking; secondhand aerosol contains nicotine, VOCs, heavy metals → ongoing concern (Calder Nicotine Tob Res 2021:1451)
5.2 Mechanism + Toxic Effects
Mechanism: nicotine → uteroplacental vasoconstriction → ↓ O₂ + nutrients; CO → carboxyhemoglobin → fetal hypoxia

Outcomes: ↓ fertility, placental dysfunction, spontaneous abortion, IUGR, preterm birth, congenital anomalies (cleft lip/palate), SIDS (major risk factor). Dose-response: 5% ↓ relative fetal weight per pack/day (DiFranza Pediatrics 2004;113:1007)
Neurotoxicity: nicotine → ↓ neuronal proliferation → cognitive impairment, auditory processing deficits, ADHD, conduct disorders, anxiety, depression, obesity
Epigenetic mechanism: DNA hypomethylation of placental glucocorticoid receptor promoter → ↑ infant cortisol stress response month 1 (Green Curr Environ Health Rep 2015;2:126)
Prevention: behavioral therapy > nicotine replacement (nicotine = neurotoxin); NRT should be avoided during pregnancy
6. Ethanol และ Fetal Alcohol Spectrum Disorder (FASD)
6.1 Epidemiology
~10% ของ women report drinking alcohol during pregnancy. FAS: 0.2–1.5/1,000 live births; FASD: 1–5% ของ school-aged children ใน US + Western Europe (CDC)
Lifetime cost of 1 FAS person: ~$2 million; US annual cost: >$4 billion (CDC)
Prenatal alcohol = leading KNOWN cause of intellectual delay — 100% preventable
6.2 FAS Diagnostic Triad + FASD Spectrum

FAS Diagnostic Triad — ท่องจำ
(1) Craniofacial: smooth philtrum + thin upper lip (<3rd %ile) + short palpebral fissures (<3rd %ile). (2) Growth deficiency: weight ≤10th %ile; persists through early adolescence; ↑ skeletal abnormalities. (3) CNS dysfunction: structural (microcephaly), neurologic (seizures — 17.7% of FASD), functional (↓ IQ, ADHD, poor memory, language delay, conduct disorder). FAS = extreme end of FASD spectrum.
FASD umbrella: FAS + partial FAS + ARND (alcohol-related neurodevelopmental disorder) + ARBD (alcohol-related birth defects)

Top 5 comorbidities: abnormal peripheral NS/special senses, conduct disorder, receptive language disorder, chronic serous otitis media, expressive language disorder (Wozniak Lancet Neurol 2019;18:760)
Other systems: smaller immature lungs + impaired pulmonary immunity (↑ infection); ↑ CHD; renal/liver/GI anomalies
Heavy alcohol: miscarriage 15% → 45%; stillbirth ×6 (Cornman-Homonoff J Matern Fetal Neonatal Med 2012;25:860)
No safe dose at any trimester — complete abstinence (AAP + ACOG + WHO consensus)
Even 1 drink/day in any trimester → ↑ behavioral disturbances in young adults
Treatment emerging: choline supplementation → benefit in animal + recent human studies after prenatal alcohol exposure
7. Pesticides — Classification, Fetal Exposure, and Toxicity
7.1 Classification Table

rows: Insecticides; many EPA-banned (DDT 1972); lipid-soluble; bioaccumulate; long half-life
Insecticides; agriculture; water-soluble; most residential use banned
Flea control + agriculture; most common food residue

blocks.text: 7.2 Fetal Exposure Sources + Biomarkers

Diet: DDT + metabolites in meat, dairy, fish, breast milk; aldrin/dieldrin in fish, shellfish. 2019 FDA food testing: residues in 57.6% domestic + 50.6% imported samples; violations in 10.9% imported (Roberts Pediatrics 2012;130:e1765)
Environmental Working Group (2004): 21 of 28 organochlorine pesticides found in cord blood of 10 babies
Biomarkers: OPs found in amniotic fluid; OPs + pyrethroids in meconium
Occupational/paraoccupational + proximity to agricultural areas = important exposure routes for pregnant women
7.3 Toxic Effects on Fetus
Parental pesticide exposure → infertility, spontaneous abortion, fetal demise, preterm birth, IUGR, congenital anomalies (Roberts Pediatrics 2012)
Chlorpyrifos + DDE (DDT metabolite) → ↓ birth weight + microcephaly
Organochlorines (DDT) → cryptorchidism + hypospadias (endocrine disruption)
OPs + organochlorines → persistent neurodevelopmental effects: ↑ cognitive delays, intellectual disability, ADHD, autism spectrum disorders

Childhood cancers: maternal pesticide use → ↑ ALL; >25 studies link prenatal exposure + brain tumors. Paternal pesticide exposure → ↑ AML (HR=2.62), fungicides (HR=2.59) — not ALL or solid tumors (Patel Int J Cancer 2020;146:943)
7.4 Prevention
Organic diet: urinary OP metabolites ↓ significantly after 5-day organic diet in children (Roberts 2012)
Wash produce; avoid animal fat + certain fish; EPA integrated pest management
Avoid maternal occupational + household pesticide exposure during pregnancy
8. Bisphenols (BPA/BPS/BPF) และ Phthalates — Ubiquitous Endocrine Disruptors
8.1 Bisphenol A (BPA) — Estrogenic EDC
BPA = plasticizer ใน polycarbonate bottles + epoxy resins (canned food lining, water pipes, dental sealants, medical devices); >6 billion pounds/year.

Mechanism: binds estrogen receptors → agonist at very low concentrations; thyroid receptor antagonist + antiandrogenic in vitro. BPA primarily unbound → active → estrogenic effects during fetal development

Animal data (below 50 μg/kg/day reference dose): urogenital anomalies, accelerated puberty, prostate/breast cancer predisposition, neurodevelopmental changes (aggression, anxiety, modified estrogen sensitivity)

Human data (>100 epidemiological studies — Vom Saal Endocrinology 2021;162:bqaa171): maternal BPA → anxiety, depression, hyperactivity, aggression in offspring. 10-fold ↑ urinary BPA → higher behavioral/emotional dysregulation at age 3

Female: infertility, recurrent miscarriage, preterm delivery, PCOS, breast cancer, chromosomal anomalies in offspring. Male: ↓ sperm count, poor motility, ↑ DNA breakage; ↓ anogenital distance in male infants of occupationally exposed fathers
8.2 BPS + BPF — Not Safer Alternatives
BPS: used in 'BPA-free' thermal paper (receipts, ATMs, boarding passes). BPF: epoxy resins. Both unregulated → marketed as 'BPA-free' without evidence of safety
In vitro: similar estrogenic toxicity + potency to BPA (Rochester Environ Health Perspect 2015;123:643). BPF active form found in placenta, amniotic fluid, fetal tissue in rats
8.3 Phthalates — Antiandrogenic EDC
Chemical plasticizers in PVC (flexibility), cosmetics, nail polish, adhesives, IV tubing, ECMO circuit. Phthalate esters NOT covalently bound → easily released → pass freely across placenta
DBP (dibutyl phthalate) levels significantly higher in women of reproductive age than in men — cosmetics, nail polish, deodorant

Toxic effects: ↓ anogenital distance, ↓ penile length, cryptorchidism, hypospadias, testicular maldescent, ↑ preterm birth, ↑ pregnancy loss, ↑ gestational hypertension (Qian Front Public Health 2020;8:366)
NICU concern: DEHP leaches from PVC IV bags + ECMO circuit → direct intravascular exposure in critically ill neonates
8.4 Prevention — Practical Plastic Reduction
BPA: avoid polycarbonate plastics (resin code 7); do NOT boil/microwave/dishwasher BPA plastics (heat → ↑ leaching)
Phthalates: PVC = resin code 3; avoid microwave in plastic; limit processed foods; avoid long-term plastic storage
Limit thermal paper (BPS) contact; consider non-DEHP IV tubing for ELBW infants
9. Organic Solvents — Lipophilic Teratogens ใน Household Products
9.1 Overview

Volatile carbon-based chemicals; oxygenated, petroleum, chlorinated classes. Found in: paint, cleaning supplies, dry cleaning, nail polish remover, printer ink, adhesives, glue. Concentrations much higher indoors than outdoors; persist in air long after use
Inhalation = primary route (volatile); also transdermal + oral. Small + lipophilic → cross placenta freely → concentrate in brain
9.2 Toxic Effects

Congenital anomalies: cleft lip/palate, NTDs, CHD. National Birth Defects Prevention Study 1997–2002: occupational solvent exposure pre-conception → 1st trimester → ↑ LVOTO, RVOTO, conotruncal defects, septal defects (Gilboa Occup Environ Med 2012;69:628)
Toluene embryopathy ('solvent sniffing'): IUGR, microcephaly, FAS-like facial features, neurodevelopmental impairment (Leibson J Popul Ther Clin Pharmacol 2014;21:e1)
PELAGIE cohort (France): maternal occupational OP solvent exposure → behavioral problems at 2 years; less severe at 6 years (Costet Environ Health 2018;17:63)
9.3 Prevention
Use in well-ventilated areas; discard partially used containers; pregnant women avoid paint fumes
Nail salon + hairdressers: limit hours, gloves + masks, maximum ventilation, cover containers, separate food areas
10. NICU Practical — Environmental History + Counseling
Smoke exposure history: active + passive/ETS + vaping; all trimesters; paternal smoking = main home ETS source
Alcohol: ถาม all trimesters รวม periconceptional period; document zero alcohol recommendation; ไม่มี safe dose

Occupation: flag farmers, painters, janitors, hairdressers, nail salon, cleaners, health workers (anesthesia/radiation). Paternal occupation ก็สำคัญ — pesticides, hydrocarbons → paternal-mediated effects (AML, Prader-Willi)
Diet: fish consumption (mercury); canned food (BPA); microwave plastic (phthalates); organic vs conventional produce (OPs)
FASD recognition: smooth philtrum + thin upper lip + IUGR + CNS symptoms → ask alcohol history directly; FASD = leading known cause of intellectual delay ที่ป้องกันได้
NICU plastic exposure: review ECMO + IV tubing DEHP content; allow hand sanitizer to evaporate before touching infant; review excipients ใน IV medications สำหรับ VLBW
Lead screening: IUGR + ↓ HC + cognitive delay → order blood lead; ask old housing/renovation/occupational history; calcium supplementation ใน high-risk pregnancy
Teratology resources: OTIS: www.otispregnancy.org; ATSDR: www.atsdr.cdc.gov
11. 5 Bedside Pearls

PEARL 1 — ไม่มี Safe Dose ของ Alcohol ในการตั้งครรภ์ — period.
AAP + ACOG + WHO: zero alcohol at any time during pregnancy, including periconceptional period. FASD = leading KNOWN cause of intellectual delay ที่ป้องกันได้ 100%. 1–5% ของ school-age children ใน US + Western Europe มี FASD — hidden epidemic. ทุกครั้งที่เห็น infant มี smooth philtrum + thin upper lip + small palpebral fissures + IUGR + poor suck + CNS irritability → ถาม alcohol history อย่างตรงไปตรงมา.

PEARL 2 — Lead ใน Bone ≠ Lead ในเลือดปัจจุบัน: Maternal Bone Lead Mobilizes During Pregnancy
Blood lead level ปัจจุบันของแม่อาจปกติ แต่ bone lead stores จาก chronic past exposure สะสมอยู่; ↑ calcium turnover ระหว่างตั้งครรภ์ → mobilizes lead → crosses placenta. Maternal trabecular bone lead = independent risk factor for cognitive delay at 24 months (Gomaa Pediatrics 2002;110:110). Calcium supplementation → ↓ lead mobilization → ↓ fetal exposure. ถามเสมอ: old housing, renovation, history of occupational lead exposure.

PEARL 3 — BPA ใน NICU: DEHP ใน ECMO + IV Tubing = Direct Intravascular Exposure
DEHP leaches from PVC IV bags + ECMO circuit → direct intravascular exposure ใน critically ill neonates. VLBW รับ IV fluids 24/7 → ↑ cumulative DEHP (antiandrogenic) exposure. Male ELBW at highest risk: ↓ anogenital distance, testicular maldescent. ใช้ non-DEHP IV tubing สำหรับ ELBW ถ้า available; หลีกเลี่ยง polycarbonate feeding bottles + BPA pacifiers; ไม่ microwave plastic ใน NICU.

PEARL 4 — Periconceptional Period คือ Most Vulnerable — แต่แม่ยังไม่รู้ว่าท้อง
Parental DNA methylation erased หลัง fertilization; remethylation เกิดที่ ~1 week = periconceptional window. ปัญหา: หญิง 88% ทำงานถึง 3rd trimester — หลายคนสัมผัส toxicants ก่อนรู้ว่าท้อง. Environmental counseling ต้องเริ่มที่ preconception visit ไม่ใช่แค่ antenatal care. ถามหญิงวัยเจริญพันธุ์ทุกคนถึง tobacco, alcohol, pesticide, solvent exposure ใน routine well-woman visit.

PEARL 5 — Organophosphates ฆ่าแมลงโดย AChE Inhibition — ทำเช่นเดียวกันกับ Fetal Brain
OPs: irreversible AChE inhibition → chronic low-dose prenatal exposure → ↑ ADHD, cognitive delay, autism spectrum disorders ที่ persist into childhood. DDT ยังถูกใช้ในหลายประเทศเขตร้อนรวมถึงบางพื้นที่ใน SEA เพื่อควบคุมมาลาเรีย. PON1 polymorphism → differential susceptibility. Prevention: organic diet สำหรับ 5 วันในเด็ก → OP metabolites ↓ อย่างมีนัยสำคัญ; ล้างผัก + หลีกเลี่ยง animal fat ลด lipophilic organochlorine.
12. Evidence Update — Landmark References
Feinberg AP. N Engl J Med 2018;378:1323: Epigenetics in human disease prevention — comprehensive review of epigenetic mechanisms and environmental influences; foundational for DOHaD framework.

Tuscher JJ, Day JJ. Neurobiol Dis 2019;132:104591: Multigenerational epigenetic inheritance — paternal TET-mediated demethylation vs maternal passive dilution; primordial germ cell reprogramming; primary source for methylation reprogramming diagram.
Isaevska E et al. Environ Res 2022;209:112717: Prenatal PM10 exposure and DNA methylation + telomere length changes in cord blood; periconceptional period most vulnerable.
Wiklund P et al. Clin Epigenet 2019;11:97: Prenatal smoking → 69 methylation sites in 36 genomic regions persist to adolescence/adulthood; meta-analysis of prospective cohorts.
Titus L et al. Reprod Toxicol 2019;84:32: DES Third Generation Study — granddaughters have higher risk of irregular menses, amenorrhea, preterm delivery; paradigm for transgenerational inheritance.
Gomaa A et al. Pediatrics 2002;110:110: Maternal trabecular bone lead = independent risk for cognitive delay at 24 months; basis for calcium supplementation recommendation in exposed pregnancies.

Chen YC et al. JAMA 1992;268:3213: Yu-cheng PCB poisoning — children born up to 6 years after epidemic as developmentally impaired as those born within 1 year; demonstrates long-term maternal body burden impact.
Wozniak JR et al. Lancet Neurol 2019;18:760: FASD — diagnosis, epidemiology, pathophysiology, management; choline as emerging treatment; comprehensive clinical reference.
Roberts JR, Karr CJ (AAP). Pediatrics 2012;130:e1765: Pesticide exposure in children — classification, fetal biomarkers, neurodevelopmental + cancer effects; primary AAP policy statement.

Vom Saal FS, Vandenberg LN. Endocrinology 2021;162:bqaa171: BPA — overwhelming evidence of harm; >100 epidemiological studies; maternal exposure + offspring behavioral outcomes; urges regulatory action.

Qian Y et al. Front Public Health 2020;8:366: Endocrine disruption of prenatal phthalate exposure — ↓ anogenital distance, ↑ hypospadias/cryptorchidism, preterm birth, gestational hypertension; figure source for phthalate exposure diagram.
Gilboa SM et al. Occup Environ Med 2012;69:628: Maternal organic solvent exposure → CHD specifically LVOTO, RVOTO, conotruncal defects; National Birth Defects Prevention Study 1997–2002.

Ha S. Curr Environ Health Rep 2022;9:263: Climate change and pregnancy health — direct + indirect impacts; oxidative stress, inflammation, endocrine disruption, psychological stress → preterm birth, IUGR, neonatal mortality.

Engel SM et al. Environ Health Perspect 2016;124:822: Prenatal OP exposure + child neurodevelopment at 24 months — 4 birth cohorts n=936; PON1 genotype modifies susceptibility; basis for genetic risk counseling.
Akinmboni TO et al. J Perinatol 2017;00:1: Excipient exposure in VLBW preterm neonates — 98% exposed; 5–9% exceed adult acceptable doses; clinical alert for NICU pharmacology.
13. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: อธิบาย Barker hypothesis และบอกว่า epigenetics เชื่อมโยงกับ DOHaD อย่างไร

ตอบ: Barker hypothesis (1990): IUGR secondary to maternal nutritional/metabolic programming → coronary artery disease in adults ('fetal origins hypothesis'). DOHaD ขยาย concept: environmental stressors (รวม toxicants) ในช่วง critical fetal development → epigenetic reprogramming (DNA methylation changes, histone modification) → altered phenotypic expression → disease across lifetime + intergenerational. Dutch Hunger Winter: offspring มี persistent ↓ methylation ของ IGF-2 loci → ↑ metabolic + CVD disease. Periconceptional period (wks 1–4) = most vulnerable เพราะ parental methylation erased + remethylation เกิดขึ้นที่ implantation.
Q: อธิบาย ion trapping ใน fetal compartment พร้อมอธิบายว่าทำไม fetus จึงสะสม toxicant มากกว่า mother

ตอบ: Fetal pH (~7.35) < Maternal pH (~7.40). Weak base nonionized ในแม่ → passive diffusion ผ่าน placenta → ionized ใน fetal compartment (more acidic) → ไม่สามารถ diffuse back → trapped ใน fetus. ประกอบกับ fetal albumin เพิ่มขึ้น progressive → protein binding ใน fetal compartment → further accumulation. ก่อน 29 wks: minimal adipose → lipid-soluble toxicants accumulate ใน brain (high lipid content) แทน. Example: DDT brain retention highest at neonatal equivalent of brain growth spurt → behavioral abnormalities (Eriksson 1984).
Q: แม่บอกดื่มไวน์ 1–2 แก้ว/สัปดาห์ตลอดการตั้งครรภ์ ทารกมี IUGR + poor feeding. ควร evaluate อะไร?

ตอบ: Approach: ตรวจ craniofacial triad: smooth philtrum, thin upper lip (<3rd %ile), short palpebral fissures (<3rd %ile). FAS triad = craniofacial + growth ≤10th %ile + CNS dysfunction. แม้ไม่ครบ FAS → ยัง ARND/ARBD ซึ่งอยู่ใน FASD spectrum. Evaluate: HC (microcephaly), MRI brain ถ้ามี CNS concern, neurodevelopmental assessment, hearing screen (serous otitis media), cardiac echo (↑ CHD), renal US. ไม่มี safe level → 1–2 glasses/week sufficient to cause FASD in susceptible fetus (ADH polymorphism modifies risk).
Q: แม่ทำงานในไร่ผลไม้ ทารกมี ↓ birth weight + ↓ head circumference. Pesticide class ที่ต้องกังวลและ mechanism?

ตอบ: Organophosphates (OPs) + Organochlorines: OPs → irreversible AChE inhibition; neurodevelopmental effects: ↑ cognitive delay, ADHD, autism spectrum (persist into childhood). Organochlorines (DDT, dieldrin) → lipophilic, bioaccumulate → ↓ birth weight + microcephaly (chlorpyrifos + DDE). PON1 polymorphism modifies OP susceptibility (Engel 2016). Biomarkers: OP metabolites in maternal urine → order if clinically indicated. Prevention: organic diet 5 days → ↓ OP metabolites; wash produce; avoid home pesticide use.
Q: อธิบายว่า BPA เป็น 'estrogenic' EDC และ phthalates เป็น 'antiandrogenic' EDC — ผลต่อทารกต่างกันอย่างไร?

ตอบ: BPA (estrogenic EDC): binds estrogen receptors → agonist; thyroid receptor antagonist + antiandrogenic in vitro. Effects: alter brain sex differentiation, accelerate puberty, ↑ prostate/breast cancer risk, behavioral changes (anxiety, hyperactivity, poor emotional regulation). Phthalates (antiandrogenic EDC): interfere with androgen synthesis + action; male reproductive tract most sensitive → ↓ anogenital distance, ↓ penile length, cryptorchidism, hypospadias, testicular maldescent. NICU: ECMO + PVC IV tubing → DEHP direct intravascular exposure. Clinical: monitor male ELBW for genital anomalies; consider non-DEHP tubing.
Q: ข้อมูลจาก Hiroshima/Nagasaki บอกอะไรเกี่ยวกับ ionizing radiation ต่อ fetus?

ตอบ: Hiroshima/Nagasaki data (Blot J Radiat Res 1975): children exposed in utero <18 weeks: microcephaly dose-dependent; lowest observable effect at 1–9 rad. ↑ Cancer incidence at ≥0.10 Gy in utero (cumulative hazard rate ~3.8× higher at ≥0.30 Gy vs 0 Gy by 1984 — Yoshimoto Lancet 1988;332:665). Mechanism: DNA double-strand breaks → impaired neuronal cell division → microcephaly; DNA mutation → malignant transformation. Cranial CT neonate: ~10.5 rad → use sparingly; prefer US or MRI; UV light does NOT penetrate fetus.
Q: อธิบาย epigenetic transgenerational inheritance พร้อมตัวอย่าง 2 ชนิด — มีนัยทางคลินิกอย่างไร?

ตอบ: Definition: epimutations ใน germline (sperm/egg) ส่งผ่านไปยัง generation ต่อๆ ไปโดย generation ที่ไม่ได้สัมผัส toxicant โดยตรงยังคง express phenotypic abnormalities. Example 1: DES — granddaughters (F3) มี irregular menses, amenorrhea, preterm delivery (Titus 2019). Example 2: Vinclozolin + methoxychlor (rat) → impaired spermatogenesis ใน male ถึง 4 generations (Manikkam 2013). Clinical implications: (1) ผลของ toxicant exposure อาจส่งถึง grandchildren แม้จะ stop exposure แล้ว; (2) history ควรถามถึง grandmother's exposures ด้วย (DES, occupational chemicals); (3) reproductive counseling ต้องระยะยาว.
Q: แม่ช่างเสริมสวย ตลอดการตั้งครรภ์. จะ counsel อะไรเพื่อ ↓ organic solvent exposure?

ตอบ: Organic solvents ใน nail salon: toluene (nail polish, glue), acetone, formaldehyde, DBP phthalate, VOCs. Route: inhalation (primary) + transdermal. Counseling: (1) maximum ventilation — open windows + exhaust fans; (2) wear chemical-resistant gloves + N95 mask; (3) seal containers when not in use; (4) limit hours during 1st trimester (organogenesis); (5) separate food/eating area from work area. Risk if heavy exposure: toluene embryopathy = microcephaly + IUGR + FAS-like features. Refer to occupational medicine.

Day 111 Preview — Fanaroff Ch.14
บทต่อไป: Chapter 14 — Fetal Growth Restriction (Fanaroff 12th ed.). เตรียมตัว: definitions (SGA vs FGR vs IUGR), placental pathology, Doppler assessment, timing of delivery, long-term neurodevelopmental outcomes — ต่อยอดจาก Day 110 (toxicant causes of FGR) และ Day 107 (Doppler) โดยตรง.
